notes: Reproduced: SKBitmap.Decode takes ~23-28 seconds to decode a tiny 8x8 PNG with a 50MB tEXt metadata chunk, versus <1ms for the same PNG without metadata. This matches the reporter's description of the decode call appearing to 'hang' on PNGs with large Exif values. The issue is in Skia's PNG codec which reads all pre-IDAT chunks in 4KB increments with no size limit or cancellation mechanism. A normal small PNG decodes instantly. Bug persists across all tested versions including main branch source.

reproductionSteps.description: Create console project with SkiaSharp 2.88.9 (reporter used 2.80.2 which lacks macOS ARM64 natives, so closest 2.x with ARM64 used)
reproductionSteps.command: dotnet new console -n Repro --framework net8.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp --version 2.88.9

filesCreated.description: Repro code that creates PNG with large metadata chunk and tests SKBitmap.Decode timing

versionResults.notes: Decode took 27.6s for 8x8 PNG with 50MB metadata. Reporter used 2.80.2 which lacks macOS ARM64 natives; 2.88.9 is closest 2.x with ARM64 support.
Decode took 23.0s for 8x8 PNG with 50MB metadata. Slightly faster than 2.x but still excessively slow.
Decode took 26.2s for 8x8 PNG with 50MB metadata. Bug persists on main branch.

actionability.reason: Bug reproduced across all versions including main. Skia's PNG codec processes large pre-IDAT metadata chunks in 4KB increments with no size limit, timeout, or cancellation mechanism. This is an upstream Skia design limitation.

proposedResponse.body:
Thanks for reporting this! We've confirmed the issue.

SKBitmap.Decode (and SKCodec.Create) processes PNG metadata chunks before pixel data in small 4KB increments. A PNG with a ~50MB metadata chunk takes **~23-28 seconds** to decode an 8x8 image, versus <1ms for the same image without large metadata. With the 100MB Exif value in the original report, it would take even longer, appearing to hang.

**Tested versions:**
| Version | Result | Decode Time (8x8 PNG, 50MB metadata) |
|---------|--------|--------------------------------------|
| 2.88.9 | ❌ Reproduced | 27.6s |
| 3.119.2 (latest) | ❌ Reproduced | 23.0s |
| main (source) | ❌ Reproduced | 26.2s |

The root cause is in Skia's PNG codec (`SkPngCodec::decodeBounds()`) which reads all pre-IDAT chunks sequentially with no size guard or cancellation point.

**Workarounds:**
- Strip or limit Exif metadata size before passing to SkiaSharp (e.g., using MetadataExtractor or Magick.NET)
- Pre-validate PNG chunk sizes by reading chunk headers and rejecting files with oversized pre-IDAT metadata
- Run decode on a background thread with a timeout to prevent blocking critical paths

workarounds: Strip or truncate Exif/metadata before decoding with SkiaSharp using a library like MetadataExtractor or Magick.NET
Pre-validate PNG chunk sizes by reading chunk headers and reject files with excessively large pre-IDAT metadata
Run SKBitmap.Decode on a background thread with a timeout to prevent blocking critical paths
